title: Moosend vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Moosend and Omnisend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your business.
intro: Both Moosend and Omnisend are popular email marketing platforms offering freemium models, but they serve different audiences. Moosend focuses on affordable, unlimited email campaigns for small businesses, while Omnisend specializes in omnichannel solutions combining email, SMS, and push notifications for e-commerce brands. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You want unlimited emails at the lowest cost with a generous free tier
You're a small business focused purely on email marketing campaigns
You need simple, straightforward automation without multi-channel complexity
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need email, SMS, and push notifications integrated
You want pre-built workflows and advanced segmentation for product-based marketing
You're willing to invest slightly more for a comprehensive omnichannel platform

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. Moosend wins on price and email-only simplicity, making it ideal for small businesses. Omnisend excels for e-commerce brands needing SMS, push notifications, and advanced segmentation alongside email. Compare based on whether you need omnichannel capabilities.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Moosend or Omnisend?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to Omnisend's $16/month. Moosend also offers a more generous free tier with 1,000 subscribers versus Omnisend's 250. However, Omnisend's pricing reflects its broader feature set including SMS and push notifications.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Moosend?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber lists and campaign data from Omnisend to Moosend. Both platforms support standard data export formats. However, you'll lose SMS and push notification capabilities since Moosend focuses on email, so evaluate whether this trade-off makes sense for your business.
